llm_reasoning: The primary limiting factor is the extremely low mean annual temperature (3.9°C), indicating a very short and cool growing season unsuitable for tobacco, which requires sustained warmth (20-30°C). Significant frost risk (16.7%) further jeopardizes crop establishment and maturity. While soil pH (5.12) is marginally acceptable, it cannot compensate for the severe climatic constraints. Operational risks include poor growth, low yields, and immature leaf quality due to insufficient heat units and frost damage. Mitigation for field-scale cultivation is largely impractical.
